codeReview.summary: This fix directly addresses the two scrutiny gaps from m3-policy-denial-and-persistence's denial round-trip spec: it lowers the OperationFailure wait bound from 60s to 15s and adds explicit A-side invariance coverage (continuous pending-operation sampling keyed by B request_id plus pre/post Sign completion count invariance). Comparing the original failing diff (b1f9835) against the fix diff (6b3fbcb), the previously missing checks are now present and wired with actionable diagnostics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Contract/shared-language drift remains around A-side pending invariance: validation text still cites total pending_operations.length unchanged, while the implemented fix intentionally enforces Sign-typed pending invariance + request_id non-admission because total queue length churns from background Ping traffic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md (VAL-POLICIES-010 evidence) says "A's pending_operations.length ... unchanged"; fix commit at src/e2e/multi-device/policy-denial-roundtrip.spec.ts:875-894 documents/implements Sign-typed pending count invariance with total pending length diagnostic-only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-worker's verification carve-out is still awkward for pure e2e-spec hardening tasks: worker correctly marked followedProcedure=false when skipping agent-browser, but this type of change has no UI-surface delta and is already validated by the edited Playwright spec itself plus project checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for session faafc265-d1f8-4b84-92b6-64792a03a51c sets skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with explicit rationale; .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md only carves out pure state-derivation/non-UI logic, not pure test-spec hardening.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton, prior failed review, and both diffs (b1f9835 and 6b3fbcb). The fix adequately closes the denial-roundtrip timeout and A-side invariance gaps identified in the prior failure, with no new blocking defects found in this scoped change.
